# How to Use Frontloom

This guide covers how to set up Frontloom in **Claude Code** (CLI, VS Code, JetBrains) and **OpenCode**.

---

## Quick Start (Recommended)

```bash
npm install frontloom --save-dev
npx frontloom init
```

The CLI auto-detects your framework, asks a few questions, and sets everything up. You're done.

### CLI Options

```bash
npx frontloom init                       # Interactive setup
npx frontloom init --yes                 # Accept defaults (auto-detect framework)
npx frontloom init --framework vue       # Skip framework prompt
npx frontloom init --tool opencode       # Configure for OpenCode instead of Claude Code
npx frontloom init --no-snippets         # Skip VS Code snippet installation
npx frontloom init --help                # Show all options
```

---

## Manual Setup

If you prefer to set things up manually, or need to understand what the CLI does:

### 1. Pick your skills

Every project needs **two skills**:

| Slot | What to pick |
|---|---|
| **Baseline** (required) | `frontloom-core-fe` — always include this |
| **Framework** (pick one) | `frontloom-react`, `frontloom-vue`, `frontloom-svelte`, `frontloom-angular`, or `frontloom-astro` |

### 2. Configure your tool

Follow the setup for your tool below.

---

## Claude Code

### Option A: settings.json (Recommended)

Create or edit `.claude/settings.json` in your project root:

```json
{
  "skills": [
    "node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md",
    "node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-react.md"
  ]
}
```

Replace `frontloom-react.md` with your framework:

```json
// React + Next.js
"node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-react.md"

// Vue + Nuxt
"node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-vue.md"

// Svelte + SvelteKit
"node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-svelte.md"

// Angular
"node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-angular.md"

// Astro
"node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-astro.md"
```

That's it. Claude Code will load the skills automatically in every session.

### Option B: CLAUDE.md reference

If you prefer to keep skills in your project docs, add to your `CLAUDE.md`:

```markdown
## Frontend Standards

This project uses Frontloom for frontend quality standards.

When doing any frontend work, read and follow:
- `node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md` (baseline standards)
- `node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-react.md` (framework patterns)

When a skill tells you to read a reference file, find it in `node_modules/frontloom/references/`.
Before finishing frontend work, review against `node_modules/frontloom/references/review-checklist.md`.
```

### Option C: Copy to project (no node_modules dependency)

```bash
mkdir -p .claude/skills .claude/references
cp node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md .claude/skills/
cp node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-react.md .claude/skills/
cp node_modules/frontloom/references/* .claude/references/
```

Then in `.claude/settings.json`:

```json
{
  "skills": [
    ".claude/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md",
    ".claude/skills/frontloom-react.md"
  ]
}
```

> **Note:** When you copy files, you'll need to update reference paths inside the skill files. Change `references/` to `.claude/references/` or adjust to match your folder structure.

---

## OpenCode

OpenCode doesn't have a native skill system, but Frontloom works through these approaches:

### Option A: Instructions file

If OpenCode supports a custom instructions file (`.opencode/instructions.md`, `AGENTS.md`, or similar):

```markdown
## Frontend Standards

Before writing any frontend code, read and follow these files:

1. `.opencode/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md` — baseline quality standards (semantic HTML, accessibility, TypeScript, UI states, security, responsive, component API design)
2. `.opencode/skills/frontloom-react.md` — React/Next.js patterns (replace with your framework)

When these skills reference a file in `references/`, read it from `.opencode/references/`.

Before finishing any frontend work, review your changes against `.opencode/references/review-checklist.md`.
```

Setup:

```bash
mkdir -p .opencode/skills .opencode/references
cp node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md .opencode/skills/
cp node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-react.md .opencode/skills/
cp node_modules/frontloom/references/* .opencode/references/
```

### Option B: System prompt

Add to your OpenCode system prompt or configuration:

```
You are an expert frontend engineer. Before writing any frontend code:

1. Read .opencode/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md and follow it as your baseline quality standard.
2. Read .opencode/skills/frontloom-react.md and follow it for React/Next.js patterns.
3. When these skills tell you to consult a reference file, read it from .opencode/references/.
4. Before finishing work, check your changes against .opencode/references/review-checklist.md.
```

### Option C: Per-conversation prompt

If there's no persistent config, paste at the start of a conversation:

```
Before starting, read these files and follow them for all frontend work:
- .opencode/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md
- .opencode/skills/frontloom-react.md

When they reference files in references/, read from .opencode/references/.
```

---

## How It Works

### Skill loading

When Claude starts working on a frontend task:

1. **Core skill** activates — it sets the baseline quality bar (semantics, accessibility, typing, security, responsive, etc.)
2. **Framework skill** activates — it adds framework-specific patterns (hooks, composables, signals, etc.)

### Reference loading

References are **not loaded upfront**. The skills tell Claude when to read each reference:

- Building a form? → Skill says to read `references/accessibility.md`
- Defining props? → Skill says to read `references/typescript-strict.md`
- Optimizing performance? → Skill says to read `references/performance.md`
- Finishing work? → Skill says to read `references/review-checklist.md`

This keeps context efficient — only relevant references are loaded per task.

### Review gate

Before marking frontend work as done, Claude follows the review checklist (13 sections, 85+ items):

1. Semantic HTML
2. Accessibility
3. TypeScript
4. UI States
5. Repo Conventions
6. Maintainability
7. Security
8. Responsive Design
9. Internationalization Readiness
10. Bundle Impact
11. Testing
12. CSS and Styling
13. Performance

---

## Framework-Specific References

Some references have framework-specific versions:

| Topic | React / Next.js | Vue / Nuxt | Others |
|---|---|---|---|
| Testing | `testing.md` | `testing-vue.md` | Adapt from `testing.md` |
| Performance | `performance.md` | `performance-vue.md` | See skill's Performance section |

All other references (accessibility, security, TypeScript, UI states, responsive, CSS, semantic HTML) contain **both React and Vue** code examples. Svelte, Angular, and Astro developers should translate patterns to their framework idioms — the principles are identical.

---

## Tips

### Always pair core + framework

```json
// Correct — baseline + framework
{
  "skills": [
    "node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md",
    "node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-react.md"
  ]
}

// Wrong — framework only (missing baseline quality standards)
{
  "skills": [
    "node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-react.md"
  ]
}
```

### Use the review checklist explicitly

For important changes, you can prompt Claude directly:

```
Before finishing, review your changes against the review checklist in references/review-checklist.md.
```

### Multi-framework projects

For projects that use multiple frameworks (e.g., Astro with React islands):

```json
{
  "skills": [
    "node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-core-fe.md",
    "node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-astro.md",
    "node_modules/frontloom/skills/frontloom-react.md"
  ]
}
```

### Override for specific projects

If a project has patterns that differ from Frontloom's recommendations, document them in `CLAUDE.md`:

```markdown
## Project-specific overrides

- We use CSS-in-JS (styled-components) instead of CSS Modules — this is an intentional choice.
- We use Zustand for all state management, not Context.
- We skip form validation on blur — only validate on submit.
```

Claude will follow project-specific overrides where they conflict with Frontloom defaults.
